message:
fix(web): defense-in-depth CRLF sanitization for header names + multipart blank-line scan

Two real correctness/hardening fixes uncovered during fresh-eyes audit of
the web layer:

1. Header NAMES were not sanitized for CR/LF, only header VALUES were.
   The HTTP/1.1 codec catches malicious names at serialization time, but
   the asymmetry meant a developer-controlled name with embedded CRLF
   reached the wire-format encoder before being rejected, producing an
   awkward error far from the call site. Sanitize names everywhere via a
   new `sanitize_header_name` helper used by `set_header`,
   `ensure_header`, the `(StatusCode, Vec<(String, String)>, T)`
   IntoResponse impl, and any path constructed via `header()`. Adds
   regression tests for each entry point.

2. Multipart `find_blank_line` tried CRLFCRLF first and only fell back
   to LFLF when no CRLFCRLF was found in the *entire* remaining body.
   When a part used a bare LF terminator and its body happened to
   contain CRLFCRLF, the parser locked onto the body's CRLFCRLF instead
   of the earlier LFLF, splitting the body in the wrong place. Fix by
   scanning for both patterns and using the *earlier* match. Adds
   regression tests for both directions of this race.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
